name: 成都
description: Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan province, is known as the 'City of Revelry' for its laid-back lifestyle, spicy cuisine, and the giant pandas. Home to ancient Shu culture, it offers relaxing tea houses, spicy hot pot, the historic Jinli Street, and proximity to the scenic Jiuzhaigou and Leshan Giant Buddha.
highlights: Giant Panda Base - Home to adorable pandas
Jinli Street - Ancient Shu culture and snacks
People's Park - Tea houses and pandas

climate.tips: Best visited in spring and autumn. Summers hot and humid but indoor AC everywhere. Winters mild but foggy. Rain common year-round. Bring umbrella.

attractions.name: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地
attractions.nameEn: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding
attractions.description: The world's largest giant panda breeding center with over 80 pandas in natural habitat settings.
attractions.tips: Arrive at opening to catch pandas most active in morning. Visit in summer for cubs. Allow 3+ hours for full visit. Shuttle bus from Tianfu Square.

attractions.name: 锦里古街
attractions.description: A reconstructed ancient street showcasing Shu culture with traditional architecture, street food, tea houses, and shops.
attractions.tips: Best in evening when lit. Try stinky tofu and sugar paintings. Combine with Wuhou Temple nearby.

attractions.name: 武侯祠
attractions.description: China's most famous memorial temple dedicated to Liu Bei and Zhuge Liang of the Three Kingdoms period.
attractions.tips: Combined ticket with Jinli Street available. Morning visits cooler. Combine with Jinli Street.

attractions.name: 都江堰
attractions.description: UNESCO World Heritage site - the world's oldest functional irrigation system still in use today, built in 256 BC.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Qingcheng Mountain day trip. Take early bus from Chengdu for full day.

attractions.name: 川剧变脸秀
attractions.description: Traditional Sichuan opera featuring the famous face-changing (bianlian) technique where actors change masks in seconds.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge. Tea included in price. Photography restricted during show. Best experience of traditional art.

attractions.name: 青城山
attractions.description: A Taoist mountain with ancient temples, forest trails, and the birthplace of China's Taoist culture. UNESCO World Heritage.
attractions.tips: Combine with Dujiangyan. Take high-speed train from Chengdu. Wear hiking shoes. Take cable car up, walk down.

attractions.name: 人民公园
attractions.description: The heart of Chengdu's tea house culture with traditional teahouses, matchmaking corner, and tai chi areas.
attractions.tips: Try a traditional tea ceremony. The park comes alive on weekends. Best to experience authentic Chengdu lifestyle.

attractions.name: 金沙遗址博物馆
attractions.description: A major archaeological site dating to the Shu Kingdom (1250-650 BC) with a modern museum showcasing gold relics.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than other museums. Audio guide essential for context. Combine with nearby Temple of Marquis.

attractions.name: 乐山大佛
attractions.description: The world's largest stone Buddha statue (71m tall) carved into a cliff at the confluence of three rivers.
attractions.tips: Day trip from Chengdu recommended. Book a tour or take high-speed train. Best viewed from boat for full perspective.

attractions.name: 天府广场
attractions.description: Chengdu's central square and transportation hub with the iconic Sichuan Museum and philosophy statues.
attractions.tips: Good starting point for city exploration. Metro lines 1 and 2 connect here. Sichuan Museum free entry with ID.

attractions.name: 三星堆博物馆
attractions.description: A mysterious ancient civilization site with extraordinary bronze masks and gold artifacts predating the Han dynasty.
attractions.tips: One of China's greatest archaeological discoveries. Audio guide highly recommended. Take bus from Chengdu's east station.

attractions.name: 宽窄巷子
attractions.description: Historic alley district with Qing dynasty architecture, now a trendy area for snacks, tea houses, and Sichuan craft shops.
attractions.tips: Try Dingding candy and sugar paintings. Best visited in evening for lantern lights. Compare with Jinli Street.

attractions.name: 杜甫草堂
attractions.description: The former residence of Tang dynasty poet Du Fu, a peaceful garden with pavilions and poetry inscriptions.
attractions.tips: Visit in spring for plum blossoms. Audio guide recommended for poetry appreciation. Combine with nearby Jinsha.

attractions.name: 成都339电视塔
attractions.description: Chengdu's iconic TV tower with a rotating observation deck and sky bar offering panoramic city views.
attractions.tips: Go at sunset for best views. The bungee jump is popular for thrill-seekers. Combined with nearby猛追湾 swimming area.

attractions.name: 东郊记忆
attractions.description: A repurposed industrial complex turned creative arts district with music venues, cafes, and art installations.
attractions.tips: Check event schedule for live music. Great for photography. Industrial chic atmosphere.

attractions.name: 琴台路
attractions.description: A cultural street famous for its Han dynasty architectural style, named after the legendary pianist Sima Xiangru and his wife Zhuo Wenjun.
attractions.tips: Best at night with lighting. Close to People's Park and Provincial Museum. Try local snacks along the street.

attractions.name: 文殊院
attractions.description: A well-preserved Buddhist temple complex known for its serene gardens, vegetarian cuisine, and cultural activities.
attractions.tips: Try the vegetarian meals at the temple. Experience morning incense and worship rituals. Popular with locals.

attractions.name: 大慈寺
attractions.description: An ancient Buddhist temple founded in the Tang dynasty, known for its large Buddha statues and peaceful atmosphere in the city center.
attractions.tips: Located near Taikoo Li. The tea house inside is popular. One of the few temples with monks still in residence.

attractions.name: 春熙路
attractions.description: Chengdu's premier shopping district with luxury malls, international brands, and diverse dining options.
attractions.tips: The giant panda sculpture on IFS roof is iconic. Great for shopping and food. Take metro line 2.

attractions.name: 四川大学望江校区
attractions.description: One of China's oldest universities with beautiful traditional architecture, lotus ponds, and historic buildings.
attractions.address: 24 South Section 3, Ring Rd, Wuhou District
attractions.tips: Best in spring for flowers. The old campus has unique architecture. Walk along the river is relaxing.

attractions.name: 石经寺
attractions.description: A thousand-year-old Buddhist temple known for its Guanyin statue and peaceful mountain location.
attractions.address: Jietai Town, Longquanyi District, 35km from Chengdu
attractions.tips: Take a taxi or bus from Chengdu. Popular with locals for weekend outings. Try vegetarian food at temple.

attractions.name: 白云观
attractions.description: A Taoist temple on scenic Wuhuang Mountain, known for Taoist culture and beautiful natural scenery.
attractions.address: Wuhuang Mountain, Xinjin District, 40km from Chengdu
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Huanglongxi ancient town. Wear comfortable shoes for hiking. Taoist culture experience.

attractions.name: 天府新区
attractions.description: Chengdu's futuristic district featuring cutting-edge architecture, themed parks, and modern amenities.
attractions.tips: Rent a bike to explore. The largest building in the world (Global Center) is here. Great for architecture enthusiasts.

attractions.name: 黄龙溪古镇
attractions.description: A 2,000-year-old water town with preserved Ming and Qing dynasty architecture, famous for its ancient streets and traditional customs.
attractions.tips: Best in summer for cooling off. Try '古镇豆花' and '一根面'. Watch fire dragon dance at night.

attractions.name: 安仁古镇
attractions.description: A historic town famous for its collections of 1940s architecture and unique museum clusters including the Liu Manor Museum.
attractions.address: Anren Town, Daying County, 40km from Chengdu
attractions.tips: Buy combined ticket for all museums. Watch 'Happy Farm' TV show filming location. Book accommodation for night tour.

attractions.name: 西羌古城
attractions.description: An ancient Qiang ethnic village with 2000-year history, featuring stone houses and terraced fields.
attractions.tips: Combine with nearby Panda Valley. Watch Qiang dance performances. Try Qiang handicrafts.

attractions.name: 月城湖
attractions.description: A scenic lake at the foot of Mount Emei with crystal clear water surrounded by mountains and ancient temples.
attractions.tips: Best in morning when mist rises. Take cable car for panoramic views. Combine with Baoguo Temple nearby.

attractions.name: 川西竹海景区
attractions.description: A vast bamboo forest on Mount Pingling with hiking trails, glass bridge, and natural scenery.
attractions.address: Pingling Mountain, Xinjin District, 40km from Chengdu
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes. The glass skywalk is thrilling. Bring mosquito repellent in summer.

attractions.name: 成都博物馆
attractions.description: Chengdu's largest museum featuring Sichuan's history from ancient Shu to modern era with over 300,000 artifacts.
attractions.tips: Free audio guide available. Best to visit on weekday mornings. Combine with nearby People's Park.

attractions.name: 成都大熊猫繁育研究基地
attractions.nameEn: Chengdu Research Base of Giant Panda Breeding
attractions.description: 世界最大的大熊猫人工繁育基地，可近距离观看大熊猫。
attractions.tips: 早上最活跃。

attractions.name: 青城山
attractions.description: 道教名山，有青城天下幽之称。
attractions.tips: 前山看道教，后山看自然。

attractions.name: 杜甫草堂
attractions.description: 唐代诗人杜甫流寓成都时的故居。
attractions.tips: 了解唐代文化。

attractions.name: 人民公园
attractions.description: 成都最古老的公园，有鹤鸣茶社。
attractions.tips: 喝盖碗茶。

attractions.name: 四川博物院
attractions.description: 馆藏文物30万件，以张大千临摹敦煌壁画最著名。
attractions.tips: 了解四川文化。

attractions.name: 文殊院
attractions.description: 成都最大的佛教寺院，有1000多年历史。
attractions.tips: 品尝素斋。

attractions.name: 金沙遗址博物馆
attractions.description: 展示古蜀文明的博物馆，有太阳神鸟金饰。
attractions.tips: 了解古蜀文化。

attractions.name: 成都美食
attractions.description: 联合国教科文组织评定的美食之都。
attractions.tips: 品尝成都美食。

attractions.name: 洛带古镇
attractions.description: 中国西部最大的客家古镇。
attractions.tips: 了解客家文化。

attractions.name: 都江堰熊猫谷
attractions.description: 大熊猫野化训练基地，可近距离观看。
attractions.tips: 比基地人少。

attractions.name: 太古里
attractions.description: 成都最时尚的商业街区，融合传统与现代。
attractions.tips: 感受成都时尚。

attractions.name: 成都夜景
attractions.description: 锦里和宽窄巷子的夜景灯光秀。
attractions.tips: 夏季夜间最美。

attractions.name: 文殊坊
attractions.description: 文殊院周边的文化街区，有各种小吃和手工艺品。
attractions.tips: 品尝成都小吃。

attractions.name: 成都啤酒
attractions.description: 成都本地啤酒文化。
attractions.tips: 品尝成都啤酒。

restaurants.name: 松云泽
restaurants.address: 6-7 Jinli West First Rd, Wuhou District
restaurants.description: Two Michelin star Sichuan cuisine restaurant showcasing authentic flavors with modern presentation.

restaurants.name: 廊桥遗风
restaurants.description: One Michelin star restaurant with stunning river views and refined Sichuan cuisine.

restaurants.name: 大妙火锅
restaurants.description: Famous hot pot chain with excellent dipping sauces and quality ingredients.

restaurants.name: 蜀大侠
restaurants.description: Popular hot pot chain known for quality meat and reasonable prices.

restaurants.name: 龙抄手
restaurants.description: Legendary restaurant specializing in wontons (chaoshou) and Sichuan snacks.

restaurants.name: 玉林串串香
restaurants.description: The legendary inventor of chuan chuan (skewer hot pot), still the most famous branch.

restaurants.name: 夫妻肺片
restaurants.description: Legendary restaurant famous for its namesake dish - husband and wife lung slices (cold beef offal in chili oil).
dishHighlights: Fu Qi Fei Pian (cold beef offal)

restaurants.name: 鹤鸣茶社
restaurants.description: Chengdu's most famous traditional tea house in People's Park, operating since 1923.

restaurants.name: 马旺子川小馆
restaurants.description: One diamond Black Pearl restaurant serving refined Sichuan home-style dishes in a beautiful courtyard setting.

restaurants.name: 听香餐馆
restaurants.description: Popular upscale casual spot known for innovative Sichuan dishes without losing traditional soul.

restaurants.name: 小龙坎老火锅
restaurants.description: One of Chengdu's most famous hot pot chains with authentic mala flavor and premium beef.

restaurants.name: 海底捞火锅
restaurants.description: Famous chain known for exceptional service, free manicures, and entertainment while waiting.

restaurants.name: 蜀九香火锅
restaurants.description: Upscale hot pot restaurant with elegant environment and premium ingredients.

restaurants.name: 钢管厂五区小郡肝串串香
restaurants.description: Legendary chuan chuan xiang spot with the best small intestine (xiaojinggan) skewers.

restaurants.name: 袁记串串香
restaurants.description: Popular chain chuan chuan xiang with self-service selection and fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: 冒椒火辣
restaurants.description: The most famous mao cai restaurant in Chengdu with intense spicy flavor and tender ingredients.

restaurants.name: 冒菜老店
restaurants.description: Local favorite mao cai spot with rich broth and affordable prices.

restaurants.name: 巴蜀大宅门
restaurants.description: Upscale Sichuan restaurant in a traditional courtyard with classic dishes and modern presentation.

restaurants.name: 红杏酒家
restaurants.description: Popular chain restaurant with consistent quality and elegant dining rooms.

restaurants.name: 转转会
restaurants.description: Upscale restaurant in a historic villa known for refined Sichuan cuisine and garden views.

restaurants.name: 成都映象
restaurants.description: Restaurant in Kuanzhai Alley serving traditional Sichuan dishes in a heritage building.

restaurants.name: 午餐钱
restaurants.description: Popular student-area restaurant known for generous portions and authentic local flavors.

restaurants.name: 蜀绣宴
restaurants.description: Upscale restaurant showcasing Sichuan embroidery culture with refined local cuisine.

restaurants.name: 老房子水岸
restaurants.description: Restaurant in a historic villa by the river, known for romantic atmosphere and classic Sichuan dishes.

restaurants.name: 蜀九香火锅
restaurants.address: 88 First Section of Yizhou Rd, High-Tech Zone
restaurants.description: Popular upscale hot pot chain with elegant environment and premium quality ingredients.

restaurants.name: 锦城印象
restaurants.description: Elegant restaurant with traditional Sichuan courtyard style, known for authentic flavors.

restaurants.name: 玉林串串香总店
restaurants.description: The original and most famous branch of Yulin Chuan Chuan Xiang, birthplace of skewer hot pot.

restaurants.name: 蜀绣香
restaurants.address: 6 Jinli West First Rd, Wuhou District
restaurants.description: One diamond Black Pearl restaurant featuring elegant Sichuan cuisine with artful presentation.

arrival.from: Major international airports, many domestic connections
arrival.tips: New airport (TFU) opened 2021 with metro connection. Also old Shuangliu Airport for some flights.

arrival.to: Chengdu East Railway Station / Chengdu Station
arrival.tips: High-speed rail network well-connected. Book early for popular routes.

metro: Line 1 to Panda Base, line 2 to Jinli
bus: Bamboo bikes (zhongbao) useful for short trips
bike: Good for short trips in center

hotels.name: 成都博舍
hotels.bookingTips: One of Chengdu's best hotels. Book far ahead.

hotels.name: 成都太古里春熙亚朵酒店
highlights: Near Taikoo Li and Chunxi Road
hotels.bookingTips: Best budget option near major shopping.

hotels.name: 成都W酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request high floor for city views.

hotels.name: 成都JW万豪酒店
hotels.address: 388 East Section of Dongmen Bridge, Jinjiang District

hotels.name: 成都凯宾斯基饭店
hotels.address: 88 Section 2, South Renmin Rd, Wuhou District

hotels.name: 成都雅诗阁来福士中心
hotels.address: 1 Section 3, South Ring Rd, Wuhou District

hotels.name: 成都熊猫王子主题酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book early for Panda Base visits.

hotels.name: 成都钓鱼台精品酒店
hotels.bookingTips: One of Chengdu's most exclusive hotels.

hotels.name: 成都锦江宾馆
hotels.address: 80 Section 1, South Ring Rd, Wuhou District

hotels.name: 成都浣花溪智选假日酒店
hotels.nameEn: Holiday Inn Express Chengdu Huanhua Stream

hotels.name: 成都望江宾馆
hotels.address: 6 North Section of Yihuan Rd, Chenghua District
hotels.bookingTips: Good for visitors to university area.

hotels.name: 成都泰合索菲特大饭店
hotels.address: 88 South Section of Shuangqing Rd, Wuhou District

tips: Chengdu is modern city - most places accept cards
But download Alipay for markets and street food

payment.description: Useful for traditional teahouses and small vendors

culturalTips.title: Chengdu Lifestyle
culturalTips.content: Chengdu people are known for laid-back lifestyle (mafan). Teahouse culture is important - people spend hours drinking tea and playing mahjong. Spicy food and chill vibe define the city.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Cuisine
culturalTips.content: Sichuan cuisine (川菜) known for numbing spice (ma la). Key dishes: hot pot, mapo tofu, dan dan noodles. Tea with food is common for digestion of spice.

culturalTips.title: Panda Conservation
culturalTips.content: Giant pandas are endangered species and national treasure. Chengdu's Panda Base is leading conservation efforts. Respect the animals' welfare guidelines.

culturalTips.title: Tea Culture
culturalTips.content: Chengdu is famous for its tea houses. Try a traditional bamboo-chair tea session in People Park. The city consumes more tea than any other city in China. Sichuan opera shows are also a must-see evening entertainment.

culturalTips.title: Chengdu Tea House Culture
culturalTips.content: Chengdu has over 10,000 tea houses. People sit in bamboo chairs (yaozi) for hours drinking tea, reading newspapers, and socializing. Traditional tea houses offer ear cleaning (掏耳朵) and mahjong. The most famous are He Ming Tea House in People's Park and Shu Kefang.

culturalTips.title: Mahjong Culture
culturalTips.content: Mahjong (麻将) is Chengdu's favorite pastime. You can find people playing in tea houses, parks, and homes at any time. The game is deeply social - it's how business deals are made and friendships are maintained. Visitors can join a game if invited.

culturalTips.title: Mala (Numbing Spicy) Culture
culturalTips.content: Sichuan cuisine is famous for 'ma la' (麻辣) - the unique combination of numbing peppercorn and spicy chili. This creates a tingling, almost electric sensation. Visitors should start with mild dishes and work up to the authentic experience.

culturalTips.title: Hotpot Social Dining
culturalTips.content: Hotpot (火锅) is a communal dining experience where a boiling broth is shared at the table. You cook ingredients yourself in the pot. It's social, interactive, and considered a bonding activity. Eating hotpot together is how friendships and business relationships are strengthened.

culturalTips.title: Slow-Paced Living
culturalTips.content: Chengdu is called 'the city where time slows down' (慢生活之都). Locals believe in enjoying life rather than rushing. You might see people playing chess in parks at noon on a Tuesday. This philosophy extends to business - meetings may start late and relationships matter more than schedules.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Opera Face-Changing
culturalTips.content: Sichuan Opera (川剧) is famous worldwide for 'bianlian' (变脸) - the lightning-fast mask changes. Actors change multiple masks in seconds, each representing a different emotion. Other skills include 'huo wei' (fire breathing) and 'chuilong' (ear pull). Shows are best experienced at Shufeng Yayun.

culturalTips.title: Three Kingdoms Heritage
culturalTips.content: Chengdu is the heart of Three Kingdoms (三国) culture. Zhuge Liang (the Sleeping Dragon) was the Prime Minister of Shu here. Wuhou Temple is dedicated to him. The city takes great pride in this era - you'll see Three Kingdoms themes everywhere from street names to restaurants.

culturalTips.title: Bamboo Culture
culturalTips.content: Giant pandas eat bamboo, but Chengdu's relationship with bamboo goes deeper. Bamboo furniture, bamboo poles for tea service, bamboo shoots in cuisine. The Bifengxia Panda Base is named after a bamboo canyon. Bamboo symbolizes resilience and flexibility in local culture.

culturalTips.title: Foot Massage Culture
culturalTips.content: Chengdu has countless foot massage (足疗) shops. After spicy food and mahjong, locals relax with a foot massage. It's not just luxury - it's a social activity where friends gather. High-quality places have private rooms for groups.

culturalTips.title: Street Snacks Culture
culturalTips.content: Chengdu's street snacks (小吃) are legendary. Try stinky tofu (臭豆腐), sugar paintings (糖画), Dingding candy (丁丁糖), and fried rice cakes (糍粑). Night markets at Jinli and Kuanzhai offer the best variety. Each snack has centuries of history.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Dialect
culturalTips.content: Sichuan dialect (四川话) is different from Mandarin. It uses unique words and a distinctive tone that makes Chinese from other regions smile. Locals often add '撒' (sa) at sentence end and use '好多' (好多) instead of '多少'. Learning a few phrases will delight locals.

culturalTips.title: Chengdu Nightlife
culturalTips.content: Chengdu comes alive at night. 339 TV Tower has sky bars, Taikoo Li has late-night cafes, and酒吧一条街 (Bar Street) near Jinli stays open until 3am. The city has a vibrant club scene with both Chinese and Western-style venues.

culturalTips.title: Panda as National Treasure
culturalTips.content: Giant pandas (大熊猫) are China's national treasure and Chengdu's biggest attraction. They're endangered with only about 1,800 left in the wild. Chengdu has multiple panda bases - the original Research Base and the newer Bifengxia location where you can sometimes see cubs.

culturalTips.title: Ancient Shu Civilization
culturalTips.content: Chengdu was the capital of the ancient Shu Kingdom (古蜀国) over 3,000 years ago. The Sanxingdui and Jinsha archaeological sites reveal a mysterious civilization with unique bronze masks and gold artifacts. This heritage is a source of local pride.

culturalTips.title: Flower and Bird Markets
culturalTips.content: Chengdu has traditional flower and bird markets (花鸟市场) where locals buy pets, plants, and crafts. The most famous is the Flower Bird Market near People's Park. It's a sensory experience with vendors, birds singing, and flowers blooming.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Hospitality
culturalTips.content: Sichuan people are known for warmth and generosity (热情好客). If you show interest in local culture, locals might invite you for tea or a meal. Saying '巴适' (bashi - meaning comfortable/great) will make locals happy. Refusing hospitality repeatedly is considered impolite.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Teahouse Social Code
culturalTips.content: Chengdu's tea houses follow unwritten rules: entering a tea house means you're open to conversation with strangers. Locals often share tables with solo visitors. The tea server (茶博士) is respected for their pouring skills - watch the water stream never break.

culturalTips.title: Building Spice Tolerance
culturalTips.content: Start with '微辣' (slightly spicy) or '中辣' (medium). Ask for '清汤' (plain) if needed. Sichuan peppercorns create a 'ma' (numbing) sensation - this is the authentic experience, not just heat. Drink tea or rice water to cool down.

culturalTips.title: Chengdu Foot Massage Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Foot massage (足疗) shops are everywhere and socially acceptable. Tips of 10-20% expected. For first-timers, '中式按摩' (Chinese style) is less intense. Popular chains include '良子' and '金色印象'. Going with friends is common.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Opera Show Guide
culturalTips.content: Shufeng Yayun (蜀风雅韵) is the most authentic venue. Book via hotel concierge or travel app. Shows include face changing, ear pulling, and tea pouring. Photography prohibited during acts. Dress code not strict but smart casual recommended.

culturalTips.title: Panda Volunteer Program
culturalTips.content: Chengdu Panda Base and Bifengxia offer volunteer programs where you can clean enclosures, prepare food, and learn about panda conservation. Book 2+ months ahead. Minimum age 12. Cost around 1000¥/day. Bring comfortable clothes.

culturalTips.title: Hotpot Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Use shared chopsticks (公筷) for raw meat. Don't stir continuously - ingredients cook quickly. Dip in oil sauce (油碟) for cooling. '七上八下' - quick dips for beef. Don't drink soup from pot - it's too oily. Split the bill with 'AA制' (AA).

culturalTips.title: Chuan Chuan Slang Guide
culturalTips.content: Chuan chuan xiang has its own vocabulary: '签签' (qiānqiān) for skewers, '数签签' (shǔ qiānqiān) to count skewers at end, '鸳鸯锅' (yuānyāngguō) for half spicy/half mild. Take your time - eating 2-3 hours is normal. Go for second round if still hungry.

culturalTips.title: Ancient Shu Writing
culturalTips.content: The Shu people developed their own writing system before Chinese unification. Some characters are still visible on artifacts at Sanxingdui and Jinsha museums. The '太阳鸟' (Sun Bird) and '太阳神' (Sun God) motifs appear throughout Chengdu's modern design.

culturalTips.title: Weekend Escape Culture
culturalTips.content: Chengdu locals love weekend trips (周末游) to nearby areas: Dujiangyan for summer cool, Leshan for Buddha, Jiuzhaigou for scenery. '农家乐' (farm stays) popular for families. Friday night traffic jams indicate everyone leaving the city.

culturalTips.title: Sichuan Tea Varieties
culturalTips.content: Chengdu tea culture centers on '三花茶' (San Hua Cha - third grade tea), '碧潭飘雪' (Bitan Piao Xue - jasmine green tea), and '竹叶青' (Zhuye Qing - bamboo leaf tea). Tea is brewed Gongfu style with small cups and a 'cha hai' (公道杯).

culturalTips.title: Panda Conservation Awareness
culturalTips.content: Giant pandas are '伞物种' (umbrella species) - protecting them protects their entire habitat. The 67 nature reserves for pandas also protect 8000+ other species. Don't use flash photography with pandas. Follow '安静参观' (quiet参观) rules.

culturalTips.title: Park Chess Culture
culturalTips.content: Chess players in People's Park are serious - some have played for decades. '象棋' (Chinese chess) more popular than international chess. Standing behind a player and offering advice is acceptable - called '支招'. It's also a form of gambling for some.

quickFacts.visa: Most nationalities require visa. 144h transit visa available at airport for eligible nationalities.
quickFacts.water: Tap water safe but use bottled water
